title: OpenTitan I2C fall-time (t_f) double-counting
hook_30s: The I2C controller holds SCL low for t_low plus the bus fall time — counted once. This one adds the fall time twice. Every clock cycle SCL is held low one fall-time too long, so the bus runs slower than configured and blows the timing budget. ChipGPT catches it with a directed test measuring the SCL low period.

steps.agent: I'll check this RTL against its spec for the t_f double-counting bug.
Reading the spec; extracting the canonicality rule.
Spec rule on line 10: t_f MUST NOT be added more than once. Now locate the RTL expression.

In i2c_scl_low the SCL low-hold duration is computed as scl_low_o = t_low_i + t_f_i + t_f_i (i2c_timing.sv:14), adding the bus fall time t_f twice. The spec requires the fall time be accounted for exactly once: SCL low period = t_low + t_f.

artifact.explanation: Spec line 10: t_f 'MUST NOT be added more than once when computing the hold duration', and SCL low period = t_low + t_f. The assertion checks scl_low_o equals t_low + t_f (t_f counted once). On the buggy RTL formal returns CEX; the correct RTL proves.
artifact.result: SymbiYosys (z3): counterexample found — bug proven formally

steps.agent: Running the directed test in the simulator.

artifact.name: tf_single_count_scl_low
artifact.stimulus: t_low_i=100, t_f_i=5; check scl_low_o == t_low_i + t_f_i (105).

artifact.title: I2C SCL low-period double-counts bus fall time (t_f)
artifact.root_cause: In i2c_scl_low the SCL low-hold duration is computed as scl_low_o = t_low_i + t_f_i + t_f_i (i2c_timing.sv:14), adding the bus fall time t_f twice. The spec requires the fall time be accounted for exactly once: SCL low period = t_low + t_f.
artifact.fix: Count t_f once: assign scl_low_o = t_low_i + t_f_i; (drop the second + t_f_i, per PR #21765).
artifact.impact: SCL is held low one full fall-time (t_f) too long every clock cycle. The I2C SCL period is longer than configured, so the bus runs slower than programmed and tightly budgeted I2C timing is violated.

artifact.coverage_status: Proven both ways: formal CEX + directed sim FAIL on buggy RTL

proof.note: Protocol timing — the natural proof is a directed test that measures the computed SCL low period against the spec model (this is how the real OpenTitan fix shipped: with a DIF test). Formal could also check the arithmetic.
